title: Scaevola pulvinaris
taxonomy: Scaevola pulvinaris is classified as a species within the genus Scaevola of the family Goodeniaceae, commonly known as the goodenia family. It belongs to the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. The family Goodeniaceae is a group of flowering plants that are native to Australia and New Zealand. Scaevola pulvinaris is a flowering plant native to Australia. It is often referred to as a coastal or beach sunflower due to its ability to thrive in dry environments.
uses: Scaevola pulvinaris is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Pacific Island region. The plant's leaves and stems are reported to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. It is also used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections. The plant's medicinal uses are not well-documented in Western medicine, but it is valued in local cultures for its perceived health benefits. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Scaevola pulvinaris for medicinal purposes.

intro: Scaevola pulvinaris, also known as Blue Porterweed, is a tropical plant species in the family Goodeniaceae, commonly found in coastal areas and islands of the Pacific. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in treating conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Scaevola pulvinaris include flavonoids, alkaloids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are utilized in traditional medicine to support immune function and overall well-being.
